teachingPoint: IR-T621-1 — T621 SagaOrchestrator MUST use storeDocumentWithOCC(versionPin:-1) at ORDER 1 before dispatching any step. versionPin:-1 signals 'document must not exist yet' — crash replay attempts to re-initialize an already-RUNNING saga are rejected with OCC_CONFLICT, triggering SagaAlreadyRunning (idempotent, not error). Plain storeDocument allows crash replay to reset stepIndex to 0, losing all completed steps.
positiveExample: storeDocumentWithOCC(SAGAS_INDEX, {sagaId, status:'RUNNING'}, sagaId, {versionPin: -1}) at ORDER 1. OCC_CONFLICT → emit SagaAlreadyRunning, return DataProcessResult.success({status:'ALREADY_RUNNING'}). No error thrown, no retry needed.
negativeExample: db.storeDocument(SAGAS_INDEX, {sagaId, status:'RUNNING'}, sagaId) — crash replay overwrites the existing RUNNING state, resetting completedSteps to 0 and losing all checkpoint data. Saga re-executes from start, causing duplicate side effects.
discriminatingConstraint: storeDocumentWithOCC versionPin:-1 at ORDER 1; OCC_CONFLICT → idempotent SagaAlreadyRunning. Plain storeDocument for saga init = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: SagaOrchestrator storeDocumentWithOCC versionPin -1 OCC_CONFLICT SagaAlreadyRunning idempotent crash replay T621 CF-19-1

teachingPoint: IR-T621-2 — T621 SagaOrchestrator MUST acquire SETNX(step-lock:{sagaId}:{stepIndex}) at ORDER 2 BEFORE the step body executes. On crash recovery, two parallel instances may both wake up for the same step. Without the lock, both execute the step body — double-charging a payment card, double-reserving stock, etc. The lock must be acquired BEFORE business logic, not after.
positiveExample: const lock = await stepLockService.acquireStepLock(sagaId, stepIndex); if (!lock.acquired) { continue; } // already locked, skip. // Then execute step body.
negativeExample: await executeStepBody(sagaId, stepIndex, payload); await stepLockService.acquireStepLock(sagaId, stepIndex); // Lock acquired AFTER body — two instances both execute body before the lock is checked.
discriminatingConstraint: SETNX step-lock at ORDER 2 BEFORE step body — lock-after-body = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: SETNX step-lock before step body concurrent duplicate execution T621 CF-19-1 crash recovery saga orchestrator

teachingPoint: IR-T622-1 — T622 CompensationEngine MUST execute compensations in LIFO (Last-In-First-Out) reverse order via [...stack].reverse(). Later steps built on earlier state — undoing step N before step N-1 ensures consistent rollback. Parallel compensation (Promise.all) is forbidden because step N's undo may depend on step N-1's undo not yet completing. Serial LIFO is the only correct strategy.
positiveExample: const reversedStack = [...compensationStack].reverse(); for (const step of reversedStack) { /* SETNX, execute, persist, emit */ }
negativeExample: await Promise.all(compensationStack.map(step => executeCompensation(step))); — parallel; order not guaranteed; step N and step N-1 compensations race. Or for (const step of compensationStack) — forward order; undoes first step before last, leaving partial state.
discriminatingConstraint: LIFO reverse order via .reverse(); serial for..of (no Promise.all). Forward or parallel = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: CompensationEngine LIFO reverse order serial no Promise.all compensationStack T622 CF-19-2

teachingPoint: IR-T622-4 — T622 CompensationEngine MUST implement stop-on-first-failure semantics: if compensation step N fails, emit CompensationFailed with failedStep and HALT immediately. Continuing after failure produces a partially-compensated saga in an undefined intermediate state — the operator cannot determine which compensations succeeded and which failed. Stop-on-first-failure is easier to reason about and recover from.
positiveExample: if (!compResult.isSuccess) { await queue.enqueue('CompensationFailed', {sagaId, failedStep: stepIndex}); return DataProcessResult.failure('COMPENSATION_STEP_FAILED', ...); } // Loop halts.
negativeExample: if (!compResult.isSuccess) { errors.push(err); continue; } // Best-effort; loop continues. Half the compensations succeed, half fail. Partial state is unresolvable without manual intervention.
discriminatingConstraint: stop-on-first-failure: emit CompensationFailed and return failure immediately. Best-effort continue = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: stop-on-first-failure CompensationFailed halt T622 CF-19-2 partial compensation undefined state

teachingPoint: IR-T623-1 — T623 ComplianceAuditWriter MUST compute retentionExpiresAt at write time from FREEDOM config key flow19_compliance_retention_days. Deferred computation (batch job runs later) means the record has no retention policy during its early life. If policy changes between write and deferred computation, the stored record gets the new policy rather than the policy in effect at write time. This breaks legal compliance requirements that use the policy at the time of the regulated event.
positiveExample: const retentionDays = await retentionConfig.getRetentionDays('flow19_compliance_retention_days'); const retentionExpiresAt = new Date(writtenAt).getTime() + retentionDays * 86400000; storeDocument(record with retentionExpiresAt) at ORDER 3.
negativeExample: storeDocument(record without retentionExpiresAt); // batch job computes expiry later. Record is in compliance store with null retention window. If policy changes before batch runs, wrong expiry computed.
discriminatingConstraint: retentionExpiresAt at write time from FREEDOM config; never deferred. Null or deferred retention = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: retentionExpiresAt write time FREEDOM config flow19_compliance_retention_days T623 CF-19-3 compliance record

teachingPoint: IR-T624-1 — T624 DataRetentionEnforcer MUST check BOTH gates before any purge: (1) retentionExpiresAt < now AND (2) legalHoldActive === false. Legal hold supersedes expiry — a record 10 years past its retention window must still not be purged if under legal hold. Checking only the expiry gate misses the legal hold. Checking only the legal hold misses the expiry window. Both are required for legal compliance.
positiveExample: if (retentionExpiresAt < now && !legalHoldActive) { /* proceed to archive-before-delete */ } else if (legalHoldActive) { emit RetentionHoldActive; skip; }
negativeExample: if (retentionExpiresAt < now) { deleteDocument(id); } // Single gate — purges records under legal hold. Legal compliance violation.
discriminatingConstraint: Both retentionExpired AND !legalHold required. Single gate = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: dual gate retentionExpiresAt legalHoldActive T624 CF-19-4 purge compliance legal hold supersedes expiry
